title: The job interview
desc: Hold your own in a formal setting
setting: You're interviewing for a job in Lucerne. The manager (using polite Sie) opens.

steps.speaker: Chefin
steps.es: Grüezi, schön dass Sie da sind. Verzelled Sie öppis über sich.
steps.en: Hello, glad you're here. Tell us something about yourself.

options.es: Gärn. Ich bi Ingenieur und schaffe sit Jahre i dem Beriich.
options.en: Gladly. I'm an engineer and have worked in this field for years.
options.feedback: Confident, relevant, and in a polished register. Strong opening.

options.en: Um, what do you want to know?
options.feedback: Shows hesitation. Have a short, prepared self-summary ready for this classic question.
Dismissive — they want to hear you speak. Give a confident summary.

steps.speaker: Chefin
steps.es: Und warum wänd Sie grad bi öis schaffe?
steps.en: And why do you want to work for us specifically?

options.es: Ihri Firma isch bekannt für gueti Arbet, und das passt zu mir.
options.en: Your company is known for good work, and that suits me.
options.feedback: Specific and flattering without grovelling — shows you did your homework.
Honest but weak — say what draws you to *them*, not just your need.
Fine but generic. Naming what specifically appeals would land better.

steps.speaker: Chefin
steps.es: Sie sind nöd vo da. Wie isch Ihres Schwiizerdütsch?
steps.en: You're not from here. How is your Swiss German?

options.es: Ich verstah's guet und lärne jede Tag dezue.
options.en: I understand it well and learn more every day.
options.feedback: Honest and confident — frames your learning as strength, not deficit. Well played.
Easygoing and positive. The 'gäll' is a nice local touch.
Too self-deprecating — you're conducting this whole interview in it! Own that.

steps.speaker: Chefin
steps.es: Das gseht mer. Mir mälded öis i de nöchste Täg, guet?
steps.en: I can see that. We'll be in touch in the next few days, alright?

options.es: Sehr gärn. Merci viilmal für s'Gschpröch.
options.en: I'd appreciate it. Thank you very much for the conversation.
options.feedback: Gracious, professional close. Exactly the note to end on.

options.es: Guet, ich warte uf Ihres Mail.
options.en: Good, I'll wait for your email.

options.feedback: Pressing for specifics can seem impatient — 'in the next few days' is a normal answer.
